[__Table with all available packages & architectures__](https://robostack.github.io/humble.html)

## Why ROS and Conda?
Welcome to RoboStack, which tightly couples ROS with Conda, a cross-platform, language-agnostic package manager. We provide ROS binaries for Linux, macOS, Windows and ARM (Linux). Installing other recent packages via conda-forge side-by-side works easily, e.g. you can install TensorFlow/PyTorch in the same environment as ROS humble without any issues. As no system libraries are used, you can also easily install ROS humble on any recent Linux Distribution - including older versions of Ubuntu. As the packages are pre-built, it saves you from compiling from source, which is especially helpful on macOS and Windows. No root access is required, all packages live in your home directory. We have recently written up a [paper](https://arxiv.org/abs/2104.12910) and [blog post](https://medium.com/robostack/cross-platform-conda-packages-for-ros-fa1974fd1de3) with more information.

## Attribution
If you use RoboStack in your academic work, please refer to the following paper:
```bibtex
@article{FischerRAM2021,
    title={A RoboStack Tutorial: Using the Robot Operating System Alongside the Conda and Jupyter Data Science Ecosystems},
    author={Tobias Fischer and Wolf Vollprecht and Silvio Traversaro and Sean Yen and Carlos Herrero and Michael Milford},
    journal={IEEE Robotics and Automation Magazine},
    year={2021},
    doi={10.1109/MRA.2021.3128367},
}
```

## Installation, FAQ, and Contributing Instructions
Please see our instructions [here](https://robostack.github.io/GettingStarted.html).

content.content:
[workspace]
name = "ros-humble"
description = "RoboStack repo to package ros-humble packages as conda packages"
authors = ["Tobias Fischer <tobias.fischer@qut.edu.au>", "Wolf Vollprecht <w.vollprecht@gmail.com>", "Silvio Traversaro <silvio@traversaro.it>"]
channels = ["https://repo.prefix.dev/conda-forge"]
platforms = ["osx-arm64", "linux-64", "osx-64", "linux-aarch64", "win-64"]

[system-requirements]
# 2.17 is the glibc version used in centos 7
libc = { family="glibc", version="2.17" }

[dependencies]
python = ">=3.11.0,<3.12"
rattler-build = ">=0.44.0"
anaconda-client = ">=1.12"

[target.win-64.dependencies]
# patch is required by rattler-build
m2-patch = "*"
# git is required by rattler-build
git = "*"

[pypi-dependencies]
# This is tipically the latest commit on main branch
vinca = { git = "https://github.com/RoboStack/vinca.git", rev = "85b4da615739af14750d759ccb4e997f83342629" }
# Uncomment this line to work with a local vinca for faster iteration, but remember to comment it back
# (and regenerate the pixi.lock) once you push the modified commit to the repo
#vinca = { path = "../vinca", editable = true }

[tasks]
upload = "anaconda -t $ANACONDA_API_TOKEN upload"
generate-recipes = { cmd = "vinca -m", depends-on = ["remove-file"] }
check-patches = { cmd = "python check_patches_clean_apply.py", depends-on = ["generate-recipes"] }
generate-recipes-emscripten = { cmd = "vinca -m --platform emscripten-wasm32", depends-on = ["remove-file"] }
check-patches-emscripten = { cmd = "python check_patches_clean_apply.py", depends-on = ["generate-recipes-emscripten"] }
remove-file = { cmd = "rm -rf recipes; mkdir recipes" }
build = { cmd = "rattler-build build --recipe-dir ./recipes -m ./conda_build_config.yaml -c https://repo.prefix.dev/conda-forge -c robostack-staging  --skip-existing", depends-on = ["generate-recipes"] }
build_one_package = { cmd = "cp ./patch/$PACKAGE.*patch ./recipes/$PACKAGE/patch/; rattler-build build --recipe ./recipes/$PACKAGE/recipe.yaml -m ./conda_build_config.yaml -c https://repo.prefix.dev/conda-forge -c robostack-staging", env = { PACKAGE = "ros-humble-ros-workspace" } }
create_snapshot = { cmd = "vinca-snapshot" }


[tasks.build-emscripten]
  cmd = [
      "rattler-build", "build",
      "--package-format", "tar-bz2",
      "--recipe-dir", "./recipes",
      "-m", "./conda_build_config.yaml",
      "-c", "https://repo.prefix.dev/conda-forge",
      "-c", "https://repo.mamba.pm/emscripten-forge",
      "-c", "robostack-staging",

toolUseResult.prompt:
Design an implementation plan for two related changes to the pj_bridge project:

## Background

pj_bridge is a multi-backend C++ bridge server (ROS2 / FastDDS) in /home/davide/ws_plotjuggler/src/pj_ros_bridge. The current branch is `unify-backends`.

### Change 1: Add pixi.toml with multi-environment support for ROS2 Humble and Jazzy

Pixi supports multi-environment via features:
```toml
[feature.humble]
channels = ["robostack-humble"]
[feature.humble.dependencies]
ros-humble-desktop = "*"

[feature.jazzy]
channels = ["robostack-jazzy"]
[feature.jazzy.dependencies]
ros-jazzy-desktop = "*"

[environments]
humble = ["humble"]
jazzy = ["jazzy"]
```

The pixi.toml goes in the package root (pj_ros_bridge/). colcon can build from the package root since it finds package.xml there. Shared dependencies: cmake, compilers, pkg-config, colcon-common-extensions, zstd, spdlog.

Tasks needed: build (colcon build), test (colcon test), clean.
Activation: `install/setup.sh` (generated by colcon after first build).

### Change 2: Remove vendored IXWebSocket from 3rdparty/

Current setup in CMakeLists.txt (lines 53-63):
```cmake
option(USE_VENDORED_IXWEBSOCKET "Use vendored IXWebSocket library" ON)
if(USE_VENDORED_IXWEBSOCKET)
  set(USE_TLS OFF CACHE BOOL "" FORCE)
  set(USE_ZLIB ON CACHE BOOL "" FORCE)
  set(IXWEBSOCKET_INSTALL OFF CACHE BOOL "" FORCE)
  add_subdirectory(3rdparty/ixwebsocket)
else()
  find_package(ixwebsocket REQUIRED)
endif()
```

IXWebSocket v11.4.6 is vendored as full source in 3rdparty/ixwebsocket/. It is NOT available on conda-forge but IS on Conan Center (v11.4.6).

New approach (same pattern as spdlog in the same CMakeLists.txt):
1. `find_package(ixwebsocket QUIET)` — succeeds for Conan builds (ixwebsocket in conanfile.txt)
2. If not found, FetchContent from GitHub tarball URL (same tarball approach used for Debian packaging per project MEMORY.md)
3. Remove `USE_VENDORED_IXWEBSOCKET` option entirely
4. Add `ixwebsocket/11.4.6` to conanfile.txt `[requires]`
5. Delete `3rdparty/ixwebsocket/` directory

The target `ixwebsocket::ixwebsocket` is used by pj_bridge_app. Both Conan's CMakeDeps and IXWebSocket's own CMakeLists.txt produce this target.

FetchContent needs to set the same cache vars as the vendored build:
- USE_TLS=OFF
- USE_ZLIB=ON  
- IXWEBSOCKET_INSTALL=OFF

### Other 3rdparty contents
nlohmann/json and tl::expected remain in 3rdparty/ as header-only — only ixwebsocket is removed.

### Files to modify
- Create: `pixi.toml` (new)
- Modify: `CMakeLists.txt` (IXWebSocket section)
- Modify: `conanfile.txt` (add ixwebsocket)
- Modify: `.gitignore` (add .pixi/)
- Delete: `3rdparty/ixwebsocket/` directory

### Constraints
- The `3rdparty/` include path must remain for nlohmann/json and tl::expected
- IXWebSocket tarball URL must use HTTPS (not git clone, to work under fakeroot for Debian packaging)
- The FetchContent SHA256 hash needs to be determined
- spdlog is already handled with find_package + FetchContent fallback in the CMakeLists.txt — follow the same pattern

Provide a detailed, step-by-step implementation plan.

{"parentUuid":null,"isSidechain":true,"userType":"external","cwd":"/home/davide/ws_plotjuggler/src/pj_ros_bridge","sessionId":"919a13f0-13fd-4936-9826-cc406d32caa1","version":"2.1.63","gitBranch":"unify-backends","agentId":"a1c25136bd7f4b676","slug":"twinkly-moseying-lemur","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":"Design an implementation plan for two related changes to the pj_bridge project:

## Background

pj_bridge is a multi-backend C++ bridge server (ROS2 / FastDDS) in /home/davide/ws_plotjuggler/src/pj_ros_bridge. The current branch is `unify-backends`.

### Change 1: Add pixi.toml with multi-environment support for ROS2 Humble and Jazzy

Pixi supports multi-environment via features:
```toml
[feature.humble]
channels = ["robostack-humble"]
[feature.humble.dependencies]
ros-humble-desktop = "*"

[feature.jazzy]
channels = ["robostack-jazzy"]
[feature.jazzy.dependencies]
ros-jazzy-desktop = "*"

[environments]
humble = ["humble"]
jazzy = ["jazzy"]
```

The pixi.toml goes in the package root (pj_ros_bridge/). colcon can build from the package root since it finds package.xml there. Shared dependencies: cmake, compilers, pkg-config, colcon-common-extensions, zstd, spdlog.

Tasks needed: build (colcon build), test (colcon test), clean.
Activation: `install/setup.sh` (generated by colcon after first build).

### Change 2: Remove vendored IXWebSocket from 3rdparty/

Current setup in CMakeLists.txt (lines 53-63):
```cmake
option(USE_VENDORED_IXWEBSOCKET "Use vendored IXWebSocket library" ON)
if(USE_VENDORED_IXWEBSOCKET)
  set(USE_TLS OFF CACHE BOOL "" FORCE)
  set(USE_ZLIB ON CACHE BOOL "" FORCE)
  set(IXWEBSOCKET_INSTALL OFF CACHE BOOL "" FORCE)
  add_subdirectory(3rdparty/ixwebsocket)
else()
  find_package(ixwebsocket REQUIRED)
endif()
```

IXWebSocket v11.4.6 is vendored as full source in 3rdparty/ixwebsocket/. It is NOT available on conda-forge but IS on Conan Center (v11.4.6).

New approach (same pattern as spdlog in the same CMakeLists.txt):
1. `find_package(ixwebsocket QUIET)` — succeeds for Conan builds (ixwebsocket in conanfile.txt)
2. If not found, FetchContent from GitHub tarball URL (same tarball approach used for Debian packaging per project MEMORY.md)
3. Remove `USE_VENDORED_IXWEBSOCKET` option entirely
4. Add `ixwebsocket/11.4.6` to conanfile.txt `[requires]`
5. Delete `3rdparty/ixwebsocket/` directory

The target `ixwebsocket::ixwebsocket` is used by pj_bridge_app. Both Conan's CMakeDeps and IXWebSocket's own CMakeLists.txt produce this target.

FetchContent needs to set the same cache vars as the vendored build:
- USE_TLS=OFF
- USE_ZLIB=ON  
- IXWEBSOCKET_INSTALL=OFF

### Other 3rdparty contents
nlohmann/json and tl::expected remain in 3rdparty/ as header-only — only ixwebsocket is removed.

### Files to modify
- Create: `pixi.toml` (new)
- Modify: `CMakeLists.txt` (IXWebSocket section)
- Modify: `conanfile.txt` (add ixwebsocket)
- Modify: `.gitignore` (add .pixi/)
- Delete: `3rdparty/ixwebsocket/` directory

### Constraints
- The `3rdparty/` include path must remain for nlohmann/json and tl::expected
- IXWebSocket tarball URL must use HTTPS (not git clone, to work under fakeroot for Debian packaging)
- The FetchContent SHA256 hash needs to be determined
- spdlog is already handled with find_package + FetchContent fallback in the CMakeLists.txt — follow the same pattern

{"parentUuid":"e224e643-f8e3-404e-bb27-55c4c73dfeb0","isSidechain":true,"userType":"external","cwd":"/home/davide/ws_plotjuggler/src/pj_ros_bridge","sessionId":"919a13f0-13fd-4936-9826-cc406d32caa1","version":"2.1.63","gitBranch":"unify-backends","agentId":"a1c25136bd7f4b676","slug":"twinkly-moseying-lemur","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_019g6rZGo359b5PHPZ1sDYk3","type":"tool_result","content":"#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Build and test pj_ros_bridge with regular, TSAN, and ASAN configurations.
# Usage: ./run_and_test.sh
set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
WS_DIR="$(cd "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)"
PKG=pj_ros_bridge

# Source ROS2
source /opt/ros/humble/setup.bash

cd "$WS_DIR"

bold() { printf '\033[1m%s\033[0m\n' "$*"; }
pass() { printf '\033[1;32m✓ %s\033[0m\n' "$*"; }
fail() { printf '\033[1;31m✗ %s\033[0m\n' "$*"; exit 1; }

# --- Regular build + colcon test ---
bold "=== Regular build ==="
colcon build --packages-select "$PKG" --cmake-args -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release

bold "=== Regular tests ==="
colcon test --packages-select "$PKG"
colcon test-result --verbose || fail "Regular tests failed"
pass "Regular tests passed"

# --- TSAN build + test ---
bold "=== TSAN build ==="
colcon build --packages-select "$PKG" \
  --build-base build_tsan --install-base install_tsan \
  --cmake-args -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release -DENABLE_TSAN=ON

bold "=== TSAN tests ==="
source install_tsan/setup.bash
TSAN_OPTIONS="suppressions=$SCRIPT_DIR/tsan_suppressions.txt" \
  setarch "$(uname -m)" -R \
  "build_tsan/$PKG/${PKG}_tests" \
  && pass "TSAN tests passed" \
  || {
    # Exit code 66 = TSAN warnings (pre-existing IXWebSocket), not test failures.
    # Check if all gtest tests actually passed.
    if TSAN_OPTIONS="suppressions=$SCRIPT_DIR/tsan_suppressions.txt" \
       setarch "$(uname -m)" -R \
       "build_tsan/$PKG/${PKG}_tests" 2>&1 | grep -q '^\[  PASSED  \]'; then
      pass "TSAN tests passed (with pre-existing TSAN warnings)"
    else
      fail "TSAN tests failed"
    fi
  }

# Re-source ROS2 base (TSAN install overlay may interfere with ASAN paths)
source /opt/ros/humble/setup.bash

# --- ASAN build + test ---
bold "=== ASAN build ==="
colcon build --packages-select "$PKG" \
  --build-base build_asan --install-base install_asan \
  --cmake-args -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release -DENABLE_ASAN=ON

bold "=== ASAN tests ==="
source install_asan/setup.bash
ASAN_OPTIONS="new_delete_type_mismatch=0" \
LSAN_OPTIONS="suppressions=$SCRIPT_DIR/asan_suppressions.txt" \
  "build_asan/$PKG/${PKG}_tests" \
  && pass "ASAN tests passed" \
  || fail "ASAN tests failed"

bold "=== All tests passed ==="","is_error":false}]},"uuid":"dc0d8826-8eb2-45d3-9b08-98f9d3160b43","timestamp":"2026-03-02T14:47:57.207Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"e224e643-f8e3-404e-bb27-55c4c73dfeb0"}

`pj_ros_bridge` uses a single WebSocket port (default 8080). Text frames carry JSON API requests/responses; binary frames carry ZSTD-compressed aggregated message data.

## Request IDs and Protocol Version

All requests may include an optional `"id"` field (string). All responses include:
- `"protocol_version": 1` - Always present
- `"id"` - Echoed if provided in request

Example:
```json
// Request
{"command": "heartbeat", "id": "req-42"}

// Response
{"status": "ok", "id": "req-42", "protocol_version": 1}
```

## Communication Overview

The diagram below shows the typical client-server interaction:

1. **Connection**: Client connects via WebSocket
2. **Discovery**: Client queries available topics
3. **Subscription**: Client subscribes to topics of interest, receives schemas
4. **Data streaming**: Server pushes aggregated binary data at 50 Hz
5. **Heartbeat**: Client sends periodic heartbeats to maintain the session

     2→

     4→  push:

     8→  workflow_dispatch:
     9→
    10→concurrency:
    11→  group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.ref }}
    12→  cancel-in-progress: true
    13→
    14→jobs:
    15→  build:
    16→    runs-on: ubuntu-22.04
    17→    container:
    18→      image: rostooling/setup-ros-docker:ubuntu-jammy-ros-humble-ros-base-latest
    19→    env:
    20→      HOME: /root
    21→      ROS_HOME: /root/.ros
    22→    steps:

    31→          max-size: 500M

    35→        run: echo "/usr/lib/ccache" >> $GITHUB_PATH

    38→      - name: Install dependencies

    44→          rosdep install --from-paths . --ignore-src -y --rosdistro humble
    45→
    46→      # Build

    48→        shell: bash
    49→        run: |
    50→          source /opt/ros/humble/setup.bash

    55→
    56→      # Test

    61→          source install/setup.bash

## Why ROS and Conda?
Welcome to RoboStack, which tightly couples ROS with Conda, a cross-platform, language-agnostic package manager. We provide ROS binaries for Linux, macOS, Windows and ARM (Linux). Installing other recent packages via conda-forge side-by-side works easily, e.g. you can install TensorFlow/PyTorch in the same environment as ROS humble without any issues. As no system libraries are used, you can also easily install ROS humble on any recent Linux Distribution - including older versions of Ubuntu. As the packages are pre-built, it saves you from compiling from source, which is especially helpful on macOS and Windows. No root access is required, all packages live in your home directory. We have recently written up a [paper](https://arxiv.org/abs/2104.12910) and [blog post](https://medium.com/robostack/cross-platform-conda-packages-for-ros-fa1974fd1de3) with more information.

## Attribution
If you use RoboStack in your academic work, please refer to the following paper:
```bibtex
@article{FischerRAM2021,
    title={A RoboStack Tutorial: Using the Robot Operating System Alongside the Conda and Jupyter Data Science Ecosystems},
    author={Tobias Fischer and Wolf Vollprecht and Silvio Traversaro and Sean Yen and Carlos Herrero and Michael Milford},
    journal={IEEE Robotics and Automation Magazine},
    year={2021},
    doi={10.1109/MRA.2021.3128367},
}
```

name = "ros-humble"
description = "RoboStack repo to package ros-humble packages as conda packages"
authors = ["Tobias Fischer <tobias.fischer@qut.edu.au>", "Wolf Vollprecht <w.vollprecht@gmail.com>", "Silvio Traversaro <silvio@traversaro.it>"]
channels = ["https://repo.prefix.dev/conda-forge"]
platforms = ["osx-arm64", "linux-64", "osx-64", "linux-aarch64", "win-64"]

[system-requirements]
# 2.17 is the glibc version used in centos 7
libc = { family="glibc", version="2.17" }

[dependencies]
python = ">=3.11.0,<3.12"
rattler-build = ">=0.44.0"
anaconda-client = ">=1.12"

[target.win-64.dependencies]
# patch is required by rattler-build
m2-patch = "*"
# git is required by rattler-build
git = "*"

[pypi-dependencies]
# This is tipically the latest commit on main branch
vinca = { git = "https://github.com/RoboStack/vinca.git", rev = "85b4da615739af14750d759ccb4e997f83342629" }
# Uncomment this line to work with a local vinca for faster iteration, but remember to comment it back
# (and regenerate the pixi.lock) once you push the modified commit to the repo
#vinca = { path = "../vinca", editable = true }

[tasks]
upload = "anaconda -t $ANACONDA_API_TOKEN upload"
generate-recipes = { cmd = "vinca -m", depends-on = ["remove-file"] }
check-patches = { cmd = "python check_patches_clean_apply.py", depends-on = ["generate-recipes"] }
generate-recipes-emscripten = { cmd = "vinca -m --platform emscripten-wasm32", depends-on = ["remove-file"] }
check-patches-emscripten = { cmd = "python check_patches_clean_apply.py", depends-on = ["generate-recipes-emscripten"] }
remove-file = { cmd = "rm -rf recipes; mkdir recipes" }
build = { cmd = "rattler-build build --recipe-dir ./recipes -m ./conda_build_config.yaml -c https://repo.prefix.dev/conda-forge -c robostack-staging  --skip-existing", depends-on = ["generate-recipes"] }
build_one_package = { cmd = "cp ./patch/$PACKAGE.*patch ./recipes/$PACKAGE/patch/; rattler-build build --recipe ./recipes/$PACKAGE/recipe.yaml -m ./conda_build_config.yaml -c https://repo.prefix.dev/conda-forge -c robostack-staging", env = { PACKAGE = "ros-humble-ros-workspace" } }
create_snapshot = { cmd = "vinca-snapshot" }


[tasks.build-emscripten]
  cmd = [
      "rattler-build", "build",
      "--package-format", "tar-bz2",
      "--recipe-dir", "./recipes",
      "-m", "./conda_build_config.yaml",
      "-c", "https://repo.prefix.dev/conda-forge",
      "-c", "https://repo.mamba.pm/emscripten-forge",
      "-c", "robostack-staging",","is_error":false}]},"uuid":"854dbcb3-47ca-4cd4-b90e-b0b641325850","timestamp":"2026-03-02T14:52:01.095Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"1be46280-6852-42ea-b2a2-7103f726c46c"}

{"parentUuid":"f1a59d26-8105-42c8-9209-f19a910ca4df","isSidechain":true,"userType":"external","cwd":"/home/davide/ws_plotjuggler/src/pj_ros_bridge","sessionId":"919a13f0-13fd-4936-9826-cc406d32caa1","version":"2.1.63","gitBranch":"unify-backends","agentId":"a1c25136bd7f4b676","slug":"twinkly-moseying-lemur","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01CdkU9y1EkN9Wfz3WznVToB","type":"tool_result","content":"#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Build and test pj_ros_bridge with regular, TSAN, and ASAN configurations.
# Usage: ./run_and_test.sh
set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
WS_DIR="$(cd "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)"
PKG=pj_ros_bridge

# Source ROS2
source /opt/ros/humble/setup.bash

cd "$WS_DIR"

bold() { printf '\033[1m%s\033[0m\n' "$*"; }
pass() { printf '\033[1;32m✓ %s\033[0m\n' "$*"; }
fail() { printf '\033[1;31m✗ %s\033[0m\n' "$*"; exit 1; }

# --- Regular build + colcon test ---
bold "=== Regular build ==="
colcon build --packages-select "$PKG" --cmake-args -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release

bold "=== Regular tests ==="
colcon test --packages-select "$PKG"
colcon test-result --verbose || fail "Regular tests failed"
pass "Regular tests passed"

# --- TSAN build + test ---
bold "=== TSAN build ==="
colcon build --packages-select "$PKG" \
  --build-base build_tsan --install-base install_tsan \
  --cmake-args -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release -DENABLE_TSAN=ON

bold "=== TSAN tests ==="
source install_tsan/setup.bash
TSAN_OPTIONS="suppressions=$SCRIPT_DIR/tsan_suppressions.txt" \
  setarch "$(uname -m)" -R \
  "build_tsan/$PKG/${PKG}_tests" \
  && pass "TSAN tests passed" \
  || {
    # Exit code 66 = TSAN warnings (pre-existing IXWebSocket), not test failures.
    # Check if all gtest tests actually passed.
    if TSAN_OPTIONS="suppressions=$SCRIPT_DIR/tsan_suppressions.txt" \
       setarch "$(uname -m)" -R \
       "build_tsan/$PKG/${PKG}_tests" 2>&1 | grep -q '^\[  PASSED  \]'; then
      pass "TSAN tests passed (with pre-existing TSAN warnings)"
    else
      fail "TSAN tests failed"
    fi
  }

# Re-source ROS2 base (TSAN install overlay may interfere with ASAN paths)
source /opt/ros/humble/setup.bash

# --- ASAN build + test ---
bold "=== ASAN build ==="
colcon build --packages-select "$PKG" \
  --build-base build_asan --install-base install_asan \
  --cmake-args -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release -DENABLE_ASAN=ON

bold "=== ASAN tests ==="
source install_asan/setup.bash
ASAN_OPTIONS="new_delete_type_mismatch=0" \
LSAN_OPTIONS="suppressions=$SCRIPT_DIR/asan_suppressions.txt" \
  "build_asan/$PKG/${PKG}_tests" \
  && pass "ASAN tests passed" \
  || fail "ASAN tests failed"

bold "=== All tests passed ==="","is_error":false}]},"uuid":"a95b3c9b-0f26-4dd8-bb83-95e6176a6a59","timestamp":"2026-03-02T14:56:02.536Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"f1a59d26-8105-42c8-9209-f19a910ca4df"}

task.prompt:
Design an implementation plan for two related changes to the pj_bridge project:

## Background

pj_bridge is a multi-backend C++ bridge server (ROS2 / FastDDS) in /home/davide/ws_plotjuggler/src/pj_ros_bridge. The current branch is `unify-backends`.

### Change 1: Add pixi.toml with multi-environment support for ROS2 Humble and Jazzy

Pixi supports multi-environment via features:
```toml
[feature.humble]
channels = ["robostack-humble"]
[feature.humble.dependencies]
ros-humble-desktop = "*"

[feature.jazzy]
channels = ["robostack-jazzy"]
[feature.jazzy.dependencies]
ros-jazzy-desktop = "*"

[environments]
humble = ["humble"]
jazzy = ["jazzy"]
```

The pixi.toml goes in the package root (pj_ros_bridge/). colcon can build from the package root since it finds package.xml there. Shared dependencies: cmake, compilers, pkg-config, colcon-common-extensions, zstd, spdlog.

Tasks needed: build (colcon build), test (colcon test), clean.
Activation: `install/setup.sh` (generated by colcon after first build).

### Change 2: Remove vendored IXWebSocket from 3rdparty/

Current setup in CMakeLists.txt (lines 53-63):
```cmake
option(USE_VENDORED_IXWEBSOCKET "Use vendored IXWebSocket library" ON)
if(USE_VENDORED_IXWEBSOCKET)
```

```toml
[feature.humble]
channels = ["robostack-humble"]
```

{"parentUuid":"e224e643-f8e3-404e-bb27-55c4c73dfeb0","isSidechain":true,"userType":"external","cwd":"/home/davide/ws_plotjuggler/src/pj_ros_bridge","sessionId":"919a13f0-13fd-4936-9826-cc406d32caa1","version":"2.1.63","gitBranch":"unify-backends","agentId":"a1c25136bd7f4b676","slug":"twinkly-moseying-lemur","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_019g6rZGo359b5PHPZ1sDYk3","type":"tool_result","content":"#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Build and test pj_ros_bridge with regular, TSAN, and ASAN configurations.
# Usage: ./run_and_test.sh
set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
WS_DIR="$(cd "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)"
PKG=pj_ros_bridge

# Source ROS2
source /opt/ros/humble/setup.bash

cd "$WS_DIR"

bold() { printf '\033[1m%s\033[0m\n' "$*"; }
pass() { printf '\033[1;32m✓ %s\033[0m\n' "$*"; }
fail() { printf '\033[1;31m✗ %s\033[0m\n' "$*"; exit 1; }

# --- Regular build + colcon test ---
bold "=== Regular build ==="
colcon build --packages-select "$PKG" --cmake-args -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release

bold "=== Regular tests ==="
colcon test --packages-select "$PKG"
colcon test-result --verbose || fail "Regular tests failed"
pass "Regular tests passed"

# --- TSAN build + test ---
bold "=== TSAN build ==="
colcon build --packages-select "$PKG" \
  --build-base build_tsan --install-base install_tsan \
  --cmake-args -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release -DENABLE_TSAN=ON

bold "=== TSAN tests ==="
source install_tsan/setup.bash
TSAN_OPTIONS="suppressions=$SCRIPT_DIR/tsan_suppressions.txt" \
  setarch "$(uname -m)" -R \
  "build_tsan/$PKG/${PKG}_tests" \
  && pass "TSAN tests passed" \
  || {
    # Exit code 66 = TSAN warnings (pre-existing IXWebSocket), not test failures.
    # Check if all gtest tests actually passed.
    if TSAN_OPTIONS="suppressions=$SCRIPT_DIR/tsan_suppressions.txt" \
       setarch "$(uname -m)" -R \
       "build_tsan/$PKG/${PKG}_tests" 2>&1 | grep -q '^\[  PASSED  \]'; then
      pass "TSAN tests passed (with pre-existing TSAN warnings)"
    else
      fail "TSAN tests failed"
    fi
  }

# Re-source ROS2 base (TSAN install overlay may interfere with ASAN paths)
source /opt/ros/humble/setup.bash

# --- ASAN build + test ---
bold "=== ASAN build ==="
colcon build --packages-select "$PKG" \
  --build-base build_asan --install-base install_asan \
  --cmake-args -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release -DENABLE_ASAN=ON

bold "=== ASAN tests ==="
source install_asan/setup.bash
ASAN_OPTIONS="new_delete_type_mismatch=0" \
LSAN_OPTIONS="suppressions=$SCRIPT_DIR/asan_suppressions.txt" \
  "build_asan/$PKG/${PKG}_tests" \
  && pass "ASAN tests passed" \

#
# * Try to find Facebook zstd library This will define ZSTD_FOUND
#   ZSTD_INCLUDE_DIR ZSTD_LIBRARY
#

if(UNIX)
  find_package(PkgConfig QUIET)
  pkg_search_module(PC_ZSTD libzstd)
endif()

find_path(ZSTD_INCLUDE_DIR zstd.h
  HINTS
    ${PC_ZSTD_INCLUDEDIR}
    ${PC_ZSTD_INCLUDE_DIRS}
)

find_library(ZSTD_SHARED_LIBRARY
  NAMES ${CMAKE_SHARED_LIBRARY_PREFIX}zstd${CMAKE_SHARED_LIBRARY_SUFFIX}
  HINTS
    ${PC_ZSTD_LIBDIR}
    ${PC_ZSTD_LIBRARY_DIRS}
)

find_library(ZSTD_STATIC_LIBRARY
  NAMES ${CMAKE_STATIC_LIBRARY_PREFIX}zstd${CMAKE_STATIC_LIBRARY_SUFFIX}
  HINTS
    ${PC_ZSTD_LIBDIR}
    ${PC_ZSTD_LIBRARY_DIRS}
)

# Some environments (e.g. conda/conda-forge, Pixi) may not ship the static library.
# Use the shared library as a fallback so consumers can still link successfully.
if(NOT ZSTD_STATIC_LIBRARY AND ZSTD_SHARED_LIBRARY)
  set(ZSTD_STATIC_LIBRARY "${ZSTD_SHARED_LIBRARY}")
endif()

include(FindPackageHandleStandardArgs)
find_package_handle_standard_args(ZSTD
  DEFAULT_MSG
  ZSTD_INCLUDE_DIR
  ZSTD_SHARED_LIBRARY
)

if(ZSTD_FOUND)
  message(STATUS "Found Zstd: shared=${ZSTD_SHARED_LIBRARY}, static=${ZSTD_STATIC_LIBRARY}")
else()
  message(STATUS "ZSTD not found")
  return()
endif()

mark_as_advanced(ZSTD_INCLUDE_DIR ZSTD_SHARED_LIBRARY ZSTD_STATIC_LIBRARY)

# Create imported targets
if(NOT TARGET zstd::libzstd_shared AND ZSTD_SHARED_LIBRARY)
  add_library(zstd::libzstd_shared SHARED IMPORTED GLOBAL)
  set_target_properties(zstd::libzstd_shared PROPERTIES
    IMPORTED_LOCATION           "${ZSTD_SHARED_LIBRARY}"
    INTERFACE_INCLUDE_DIRECTORIES "${ZSTD_INCLUDE_DIR}"
  )
endif()

if(NOT TARGET zstd::libzstd_static AND ZSTD_STATIC_LIBRARY)
  add_library(zstd::libzstd_static STATIC IMPORTED GLOBAL)
  set_target_properties(zstd::libzstd_static PROPERTIES
    IMPORTED_LOCATION           "${ZSTD_STATIC_LIBRARY}"
    INTERFACE_INCLUDE_DIRECTORIES "${ZSTD_INCLUDE_DIR}"
  )
endif()","is_error":false}]},"uuid":"9d616edb-04a7-47cb-a9b2-8728f7145f3c","timestamp":"2026-03-02T14:48:00.618Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"96513e77-621a-43e6-9097-af2d5e1c67e2"}

{"parentUuid":"2428a68e-ec55-475b-9482-2e6ab38d69ff","isSidechain":true,"userType":"external","cwd":"/home/davide/ws_plotjuggler/src/pj_ros_bridge","sessionId":"919a13f0-13fd-4936-9826-cc406d32caa1","version":"2.1.63","gitBranch":"unify-backends","agentId":"a1c25136bd7f4b676","slug":"twinkly-moseying-lemur","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01MWCVir6UeQTMURTwr9AWWu","type":"tool_result","content":"# API Protocol

`pj_ros_bridge` uses a single WebSocket port (default 8080). Text frames carry JSON API requests/responses; binary frames carry ZSTD-compressed aggregated message data.

## Request IDs and Protocol Version

All requests may include an optional `"id"` field (string). All responses include:
- `"protocol_version": 1` - Always present
- `"id"` - Echoed if provided in request

Example:
```json
// Request
{"command": "heartbeat", "id": "req-42"}

// Response
{"status": "ok", "id": "req-42", "protocol_version": 1}
```

## Communication Overview

The diagram below shows the typical client-server interaction:

1. **Connection**: Client connects via WebSocket
2. **Discovery**: Client queries available topics
3. **Subscription**: Client subscribes to topics of interest, receives schemas
4. **Data streaming**: Server pushes aggregated binary data at 50 Hz
5. **Heartbeat**: Client sends periodic heartbeats to maintain the session

    10→concurrency:

    12→  cancel-in-progress: true

on:
  push:
    branches: [ development, main ]
  pull_request:
    branches: [ development, main ]
  workflow_dispatch:

concurrency:
  group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: true

jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-24.04
    container:
      image: rostooling/setup-ros-docker:ubuntu-noble-ros-rolling-ros-base-latest
    env:
      HOME: /root
      ROS_HOME: /root/.ros
    steps:
      # Checkout repository
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      # Setup ccache for faster rebuilds
      - name: Setup ccache
        uses: hendrikmuhs/ccache-action@v1.2
        with:
          key: ${{ github.job }}-rolling
          max-size: 500M

      # Configure ccache for colcon
      - name: Configure ccache
        run: echo "/usr/lib/ccache" >> $GITHUB_PATH

      # Install dependencies via rosdep
      - name: Install dependencies
        shell: bash
        run: |
          source /opt/ros/rolling/setup.bash
          apt-get update
          rosdep update --rosdistro rolling
          rosdep install --from-paths . --ignore-src -y --rosdistro rolling

      # Build
      - name: Build
        shell: bash
        run: |
          source /opt/ros/rolling/setup.bash
          colcon build --packages-select pj_ros_bridge \
            --cmake-args -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release \
            -DCMAKE_C_COMPILER_LAUNCHER=ccache \
            -DCMAKE_CXX_COMPILER_LAUNCHER=ccache

      # Test
      - name: Test
        shell: bash
        run: |
          source /opt/ros/rolling/setup.bash
          source install/setup.bash
          colcon test --packages-select pj_ros_bridge
          colcon test-result --verbose","is_error":false}]},"uuid":"5ddb99db-d999-4ed9-bed8-fbcc716f848c","timestamp":"2026-03-02T14:49:21.590Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"27ff737b-0bc8-4d23-82aa-cbff0a9f30d3"}

## Why ROS and Conda?
Welcome to RoboStack, which tightly couples ROS with Conda, a cross-platform, language-agnostic package manager. We provide ROS binaries for Linux, macOS, Windows and ARM (Linux). Installing other recent packages via conda-forge side-by-side works easily, e.g. you can install TensorFlow/PyTorch in the same environment as ROS humble without any issues. As no system libraries are used, you can also easily install ROS humble on any recent Linux Distribution - including older versions of Ubuntu. As the packages are pre-built, it saves you from compiling from source, which is especially helpful on macOS and Windows. No root access is required, all packages live in your home directory. We have recently written up a [paper](https://arxiv.org/abs/2104.12910) and [blog post](https://medium.com/robostack/cross-platform-conda-packages-for-ros-fa1974fd1de3) with more information.

## Attribution
If you use RoboStack in your academic work, please refer to the following paper:
```bibtex
@article{FischerRAM2021,
    title={A RoboStack Tutorial: Using the Robot Operating System Alongside the Conda and Jupyter Data Science Ecosystems},
    author={Tobias Fischer and Wolf Vollprecht and Silvio Traversaro and Sean Yen and Carlos Herrero and Michael Milford},
    journal={IEEE Robotics and Automation Magazine},
    year={2021},
    doi={10.1109/MRA.2021.3128367},
}
```

name = "ros-humble"
description = "RoboStack repo to package ros-humble packages as conda packages"
authors = ["Tobias Fischer <tobias.fischer@qut.edu.au>", "Wolf Vollprecht <w.vollprecht@gmail.com>", "Silvio Traversaro <silvio@traversaro.it>"]
channels = ["https://repo.prefix.dev/conda-forge"]
platforms = ["osx-arm64", "linux-64", "osx-64", "linux-aarch64", "win-64"]

[system-requirements]
# 2.17 is the glibc version used in centos 7
libc = { family="glibc", version="2.17" }

[dependencies]
python = ">=3.11.0,<3.12"
rattler-build = ">=0.44.0"
anaconda-client = ">=1.12"

[target.win-64.dependencies]
# patch is required by rattler-build
m2-patch = "*"
# git is required by rattler-build
git = "*"

[pypi-dependencies]
# This is tipically the latest commit on main branch
vinca = { git = "https://github.com/RoboStack/vinca.git", rev = "85b4da615739af14750d759ccb4e997f83342629" }
# Uncomment this line to work with a local vinca for faster iteration, but remember to comment it back
# (and regenerate the pixi.lock) once you push the modified commit to the repo
#vinca = { path = "../vinca", editable = true }

[tasks]
upload = "anaconda -t $ANACONDA_API_TOKEN upload"
generate-recipes = { cmd = "vinca -m", depends-on = ["remove-file"] }
check-patches = { cmd = "python check_patches_clean_apply.py", depends-on = ["generate-recipes"] }
generate-recipes-emscripten = { cmd = "vinca -m --platform emscripten-wasm32", depends-on = ["remove-file"] }
check-patches-emscripten = { cmd = "python check_patches_clean_apply.py", depends-on = ["generate-recipes-emscripten"] }
remove-file = { cmd = "rm -rf recipes; mkdir recipes" }
build = { cmd = "rattler-build build --recipe-dir ./recipes -m ./conda_build_config.yaml -c https://repo.prefix.dev/conda-forge -c robostack-staging  --skip-existing", depends-on = ["generate-recipes"] }
build_one_package = { cmd = "cp ./patch/$PACKAGE.*patch ./recipes/$PACKAGE/patch/; rattler-build build --recipe ./recipes/$PACKAGE/recipe.yaml -m ./conda_build_config.yaml -c https://repo.prefix.dev/conda-forge -c robostack-staging", env = { PACKAGE = "ros-humble-ros-workspace" } }
create_snapshot = { cmd = "vinca-snapshot" }


[tasks.build-emscripten]
  cmd = [
      "rattler-build", "build",
      "--package-format", "tar-bz2",
      "--recipe-dir", "./recipes",
      "-m", "./conda_build_config.yaml",
      "-c", "https://repo.prefix.dev/conda-forge",
      "-c", "https://repo.mamba.pm/emscripten-forge",
      "-c", "robostack-staging",","is_error":false}]},"uuid":"854dbcb3-47ca-4cd4-b90e-b0b641325850","timestamp":"2026-03-02T14:52:01.095Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"1be46280-6852-42ea-b2a2-7103f726c46c"}

{"parentUuid":"fc88b8ef-04c7-41e7-ba75-3e6cd181e995","isSidechain":true,"userType":"external","cwd":"/home/davide/ws_plotjuggler/src/pj_ros_bridge","sessionId":"919a13f0-13fd-4936-9826-cc406d32caa1","version":"2.1.63","gitBranch":"unify-backends","agentId":"a1c25136bd7f4b676","slug":"twinkly-moseying-lemur","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_012mkGy8tuR3k7ycbduVKB8W","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→cmake_minimum_required(VERSION 3.14)
     2→project(pj_bridge)
     3→
     4→# Compiler settings
     5→if(CMAKE_COMPILER_IS_GNUCXX OR CMAKE_CXX_COMPILER_ID MATCHES "Clang")
     6→  add_compile_options(-Wall -Wextra -Wpedantic)
     7→endif()
     8→
     9→set(CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD 17)
    10→set(CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD_REQUIRED ON)
    11→
    12→# Sanitizer options
    13→option(ENABLE_TSAN "Enable Thread Sanitizer" OFF)
    14→if(ENABLE_TSAN)
    15→  add_compile_options(-fsanitize=thread -g -O1)
    16→  add_link_options(-fsanitize=thread)
    17→endif()
    18→
    19→option(ENABLE_ASAN "Enable Address Sanitizer" OFF)
    20→if(ENABLE_ASAN)
    21→  add_compile_options(-fsanitize=address -fno-omit-frame-pointer -g -O1)
    22→  add_link_options(-fsanitize=address)
    23→endif()
    24→
    25→# Custom cmake modules
    26→list(APPEND CMAKE_MODULE_PATH ${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/cmake)
    27→
    28→# ============================================================================
    29→# Backend detection
    30→# ============================================================================
    31→find_package(ament_cmake QUIET)
    32→# RTI Connext DDS — only look for it if explicitly requested or available
    33→option(ENABLE_RTI "Build RTI Connext DDS backend" OFF)
    34→
    35→if(ament_cmake_FOUND)
    36→  message(STATUS "ROS2 (ament_cmake) found — will build pj_bridge_ros2")
    37→endif()
    38→
    39→if(ENABLE_RTI)
    40→  find_package(RTIConnextDDS QUIET)
    41→  if(RTIConnextDDS_FOUND)
    42→    message(STATUS "RTI Connext DDS found — will build pj_bridge_rti")
    43→  else()
    44→    message(WARNING "ENABLE_RTI=ON but RTIConnextDDS not found — skipping RTI backend")
    45→  endif()
    46→endif()
    47→
    48→# ============================================================================
    49→# Common dependencies (for app layer)
    50→# ============================================================================
    51→find_package(ZSTD REQUIRED)
    52→
    53→# IXWebSocket (vendored in 3rdparty/)
    54→option(USE_VENDORED_IXWEBSOCKET "Use vendored IXWebSocket library" ON)
    55→if(USE_VENDORED_IXWEBSOCKET)
    56→  message(STATUS "Using vendored IXWebSocket (3rdparty/ixwebsocket)")
    57→  set(USE_TLS OFF CACHE BOOL "" FORCE)
    58→  set(USE_ZLIB ON CACHE BOOL "" FORCE)
    59→  set(IXWEBSOCKET_INSTALL OFF CACHE BOOL "" FORCE)
    60→  add_subdirectory(3rdparty/ixwebsocket)
    61→else()
    62→  find_package(ixwebsocket REQUIRED)
    63→endif()
    64→
    65→# spdlog — prefer system package (required for ROS2 ABI compatibility with
    66→# librcl_logging_spdlog), fall back to FetchContent for standalone builds.
    67→include(FetchContent)
    68→find_package(spdlog QUIET)
    69→if(NOT spdlog_FOUND)
    70→  message(STATUS "System spdlog not found — fetching via FetchContent")
    71→  FetchContent_Declare(
    72→    spdlog
    73→    URL https://github.com/gabime/spdlog/archive/refs/tags/v1.15.0.tar.gz
    74→    URL_HASH SHA256=9962648c9b4f1a7bbc76fd8d9172555bad1871fdb14ff4f842ef87949682caa5
    75→  )
    76→  set(SPDLOG_INSTALL OFF CACHE BOOL "" FORCE)
    77→  FetchContent_MakeAvailable(spdlog)
    78→else()
    79→  message(STATUS "Using system spdlog ${spdlog_VERSION}")
    80→endif()
    81→
    82→# ============================================================================
    83→# Data path for tests
    84→# ============================================================================
    85→get_filename_component(DATA_PATH "${CMAKE_CURRENT_LIST_DIR}/DATA" ABSOLUTE)
    86→configure_file(data_path.hpp.in "${CMAKE_BINARY_DIR}/include/data_path.hpp" @ONLY)
    87→add_library(data_path INTERFACE IMPORTED)
    88→target_include_directories(data_path INTERFACE ${CMAKE_BINARY_DIR}/include)
    89→
    90→# ============================================================================
    91→# pj_bridge_app — backend-agnostic core library
    92→# ============================================================================
    93→add_library(pj_bridge_app STATIC
    94→  app/src/middleware/websocket_middleware.cpp
    95→  app/src/message_buffer.cpp
    96→  app/src/session_manager.cpp
    97→  app/src/message_serializer.cpp
    98→  app/src/bridge_server.cpp
    99→  app/src/standalone_event_loop.cpp
   100→)
   101→
   102→target_include_directories(pj_bridge_app PUBLIC
   103→  ${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/app/include
   104→  ${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/3rdparty
   105→)
   106→
   107→target_link_libraries(pj_bridge_app PUBLIC
   108→  zstd::libzstd_shared
   109→  ixwebsocket::ixwebsocket
   110→  spdlog::spdlog
   111→)
   112→
   113→# ============================================================================
   114→# pj_bridge_ros2 — ROS2 backend (only if ament_cmake found)
   115→# ============================================================================
   116→if(ament_cmake_FOUND)
   117→  find_package(ament_index_cpp REQUIRED)

   119→  find_package(sensor_msgs REQUIRED)
   120→  find_package(nav_msgs REQUIRED)
   121→
   122→  # ROS2 plugin library (sources shared between executable and tests)

   129→    ros2/src/ros2_subscription_manager.cpp
   130→  )
   131→
   132→  target_include_directories(pj_bridge_ros2_lib PUBLIC
   133→    ${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/ros2/include
   134→  )
   135→
   136→  target_link_libraries(pj_bridge_ros2_lib PUBLIC
   137→    pj_bridge_app
   138→  )
   139→
   140→  ament_target_dependencies(pj_bridge_ros2_lib PUBLIC
   141→    ament_index_cpp
   142→    rclcpp
   143→    sensor_msgs
   144→    nav_msgs
   145→  )
   146→
   147→  # ROS2 executable
   148→  add_executable(pj_bridge_ros2
   149→    ros2/src/main.cpp
   150→  )
   151→
   152→  target_link_libraries(pj_bridge_ros2
   153→    pj_bridge_ros2_lib
   154→  )
   155→
   156→  ament_target_dependencies(pj_bridge_ros2
   157→    rclcpp
   158→  )
   159→
   160→  # Install ROS2 executable
   161→  install(TARGETS pj_bridge_ros2
   162→    DESTINATION lib/${PROJECT_NAME}
   163→  )

{"parentUuid":"f1a59d26-8105-42c8-9209-f19a910ca4df","isSidechain":true,"userType":"external","cwd":"/home/davide/ws_plotjuggler/src/pj_ros_bridge","sessionId":"919a13f0-13fd-4936-9826-cc406d32caa1","version":"2.1.63","gitBranch":"unify-backends","agentId":"a1c25136bd7f4b676","slug":"twinkly-moseying-lemur","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01CdkU9y1EkN9Wfz3WznVToB","type":"tool_result","content":"#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Build and test pj_ros_bridge with regular, TSAN, and ASAN configurations.
# Usage: ./run_and_test.sh
set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
WS_DIR="$(cd "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)"
PKG=pj_ros_bridge

# Source ROS2
source /opt/ros/humble/setup.bash

cd "$WS_DIR"

bold() { printf '\033[1m%s\033[0m\n' "$*"; }
pass() { printf '\033[1;32m✓ %s\033[0m\n' "$*"; }
fail() { printf '\033[1;31m✗ %s\033[0m\n' "$*"; exit 1; }

# --- Regular build + colcon test ---
bold "=== Regular build ==="
colcon build --packages-select "$PKG" --cmake-args -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release

bold "=== Regular tests ==="
colcon test --packages-select "$PKG"
colcon test-result --verbose || fail "Regular tests failed"
pass "Regular tests passed"

# --- TSAN build + test ---
bold "=== TSAN build ==="
colcon build --packages-select "$PKG" \
  --build-base build_tsan --install-base install_tsan \
  --cmake-args -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release -DENABLE_TSAN=ON

bold "=== TSAN tests ==="
source install_tsan/setup.bash
TSAN_OPTIONS="suppressions=$SCRIPT_DIR/tsan_suppressions.txt" \
  setarch "$(uname -m)" -R \
  "build_tsan/$PKG/${PKG}_tests" \
  && pass "TSAN tests passed" \
  || {
    # Exit code 66 = TSAN warnings (pre-existing IXWebSocket), not test failures.
    # Check if all gtest tests actually passed.
    if TSAN_OPTIONS="suppressions=$SCRIPT_DIR/tsan_suppressions.txt" \
       setarch "$(uname -m)" -R \
       "build_tsan/$PKG/${PKG}_tests" 2>&1 | grep -q '^\[  PASSED  \]'; then
      pass "TSAN tests passed (with pre-existing TSAN warnings)"
    else
      fail "TSAN tests failed"
    fi
  }

# Re-source ROS2 base (TSAN install overlay may interfere with ASAN paths)
source /opt/ros/humble/setup.bash

# --- ASAN build + test ---
bold "=== ASAN build ==="
colcon build --packages-select "$PKG" \
  --build-base build_asan --install-base install_asan \
  --cmake-args -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release -DENABLE_ASAN=ON

bold "=== ASAN tests ==="
source install_asan/setup.bash
ASAN_OPTIONS="new_delete_type_mismatch=0" \
LSAN_OPTIONS="suppressions=$SCRIPT_DIR/asan_suppressions.txt" \
  "build_asan/$PKG/${PKG}_tests" \
  && pass "ASAN tests passed" \
  || fail "ASAN tests failed"

bold "=== All tests passed ==="","is_error":false}]},"uuid":"a95b3c9b-0f26-4dd8-bb83-95e6176a6a59","timestamp":"2026-03-02T14:56:02.536Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"f1a59d26-8105-42c8-9209-f19a910ca4df"}

file.content:
cmake_minimum_required(VERSION 3.14)
project(pj_bridge)

# Compiler settings
if(CMAKE_COMPILER_IS_GNUCXX OR CMAKE_CXX_COMPILER_ID MATCHES "Clang")
  add_compile_options(-Wall -Wextra -Wpedantic)
endif()

set(CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD 17)
set(CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD_REQUIRED ON)

# Sanitizer options
option(ENABLE_TSAN "Enable Thread Sanitizer" OFF)
if(ENABLE_TSAN)
  add_compile_options(-fsanitize=thread -g -O1)
  add_link_options(-fsanitize=thread)
endif()

option(ENABLE_ASAN "Enable Address Sanitizer" OFF)
if(ENABLE_ASAN)
  add_compile_options(-fsanitize=address -fno-omit-frame-pointer -g -O1)
  add_link_options(-fsanitize=address)
endif()

# Custom cmake modules
list(APPEND CMAKE_MODULE_PATH ${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/cmake)

# ============================================================================
# Backend detection
# ============================================================================
find_package(ament_cmake QUIET)
# RTI Connext DDS — only look for it if explicitly requested or available
option(ENABLE_RTI "Build RTI Connext DDS backend" OFF)

if(ament_cmake_FOUND)
  message(STATUS "ROS2 (ament_cmake) found — will build pj_bridge_ros2")
endif()

if(ENABLE_RTI)
  find_package(RTIConnextDDS QUIET)
  if(RTIConnextDDS_FOUND)
    message(STATUS "RTI Connext DDS found — will build pj_bridge_rti")
  else()
    message(WARNING "ENABLE_RTI=ON but RTIConnextDDS not found — skipping RTI backend")
  endif()
endif()

# ============================================================================
# Common dependencies (for app layer)
# ============================================================================
find_package(ZSTD REQUIRED)

# IXWebSocket (vendored in 3rdparty/)
option(USE_VENDORED_IXWEBSOCKET "Use vendored IXWebSocket library" ON)
if(USE_VENDORED_IXWEBSOCKET)
  message(STATUS "Using vendored IXWebSocket (3rdparty/ixwebsocket)")
  set(USE_TLS OFF CACHE BOOL "" FORCE)
  set(USE_ZLIB ON CACHE BOOL "" FORCE)
  set(IXWEBSOCKET_INSTALL OFF CACHE BOOL "" FORCE)
  add_subdirectory(3rdparty/ixwebsocket)
else()
  find_package(ixwebsocket REQUIRED)
endif()

# spdlog — prefer system package (required for ROS2 ABI compatibility with
# librcl_logging_spdlog), fall back to FetchContent for standalone builds.
include(FetchContent)
find_package(spdlog QUIET)
if(NOT spdlog_FOUND)
  message(STATUS "System spdlog not found — fetching via FetchContent")
  FetchContent_Declare(
    spdlog
    URL https://github.com/gabime/spdlog/archive/refs/tags/v1.15.0.tar.gz
    URL_HASH SHA256=9962648c9b4f1a7bbc76fd8d9172555bad1871fdb14ff4f842ef87949682caa5
  )
  set(SPDLOG_INSTALL OFF CACHE BOOL "" FORCE)
  FetchContent_MakeAvailable(spdlog)
else()
  message(STATUS "Using system spdlog ${spdlog_VERSION}")
endif()

# ============================================================================
# Data path for tests
# ============================================================================
get_filename_component(DATA_PATH "${CMAKE_CURRENT_LIST_DIR}/DATA" ABSOLUTE)
configure_file(data_path.hpp.in "${CMAKE_BINARY_DIR}/include/data_path.hpp" @ONLY)
add_library(data_path INTERFACE IMPORTED)
target_include_directories(data_path INTERFACE ${CMAKE_BINARY_DIR}/include)

# ============================================================================
# pj_bridge_app — backend-agnostic core library
# ============================================================================
add_library(pj_bridge_app STATIC
  app/src/middleware/websocket_middleware.cpp
  app/src/message_buffer.cpp
  app/src/session_manager.cpp
  app/src/message_serializer.cpp
  app/src/bridge_server.cpp
  app/src/standalone_event_loop.cpp
)

input.content:
# Plan: Add Pixi support (Humble/Jazzy) + Remove vendored IXWebSocket

## Context

PR #2 introduced pixi support but only for Jazzy. We want multi-environment pixi support for both Humble and Jazzy ROS2 builds, and we want to remove the vendored IXWebSocket from `3rdparty/` now that it's available on both conda-forge (v11.4.6) and Conan Center (v11.4.6).

## Step 1: Create `pixi.toml` with Humble/Jazzy environments

Create `pixi.toml` in the project root with:

- **Workspace**: `conda-forge` base channel, `linux-64` platform
- **Shared dependencies**: `cmake`, `compilers`, `pkg-config`, `ninja`, `colcon-common-extensions`, `zstd`, `spdlog`, `ixwebsocket`
- **Feature `humble`**: channel `robostack-humble`, dep `ros-humble-desktop`
- **Feature `jazzy`**: channel `robostack-jazzy`, dep `ros-jazzy-desktop`
- **Environments**: `humble = ["humble"]`, `jazzy = ["jazzy"]`
- **Activation**: `scripts = ["install/setup.sh"]` (generated after first build)
- **Tasks**: `build` (colcon build), `test` (colcon test + test-result), `clean`

Usage: `pixi run -e humble build` or `pixi run -e jazzy build`

## Step 2: Remove vendored IXWebSocket, use find_package + FetchContent fallback

**File: `CMakeLists.txt`** (lines 53-63)

Replace the `USE_VENDORED_IXWEBSOCKET` option block with the same pattern used for spdlog (lines 65-80):

```cmake
# IXWebSocket — prefer system/pixi/Conan package, FetchContent fallback for bare builds
find_package(ixwebsocket QUIET)
if(NOT ixwebsocket_FOUND)
  message(STATUS "ixwebsocket not found — fetching via FetchContent")
  FetchContent_Declare(
    ixwebsocket
    URL https://github.com/machinezone/IXWebSocket/archive/refs/tags/v11.4.6.tar.gz
    URL_HASH SHA256=<to-be-computed>
  )
  set(USE_TLS OFF CACHE BOOL "" FORCE)
  set(USE_ZLIB ON CACHE BOOL "" FORCE)
  set(IXWEBSOCKET_INSTALL OFF CACHE BOOL "" FORCE)
  FetchContent_MakeAvailable(ixwebsocket)
else()
  message(STATUS "Using system ixwebsocket")
endif()
```

Move `include(FetchContent)` above the ixwebsocket block (currently it's on line 67, between ixwebsocket and spdlog).

The target `ixwebsocket::ixwebsocket` (line 109) works in all three cases:
- **Pixi**: conda-forge `ixwebsocket` package provides cmake config
- **Conan**: CMakeDeps generates the target
- **FetchContent**: IXWebSocket's own CMakeLists.txt creates the alias

## Step 3: Add ixwebsocket to `conanfile.txt`

**File: `conanfile.txt`**

Add `ixwebsocket/11.4.6` to `[requires]` so the FastDDS Conan build finds it via `find_package`:

```
[requires]
fast-dds/3.4.0
cli11/2.6.0
ixwebsocket/11.4.6

[generators]
CMakeDeps
CMakeToolchain
```

## Step 4: Delete vendored IXWebSocket

Delete `3rdparty/ixwebsocket/` directory. The `3rdparty/` directory itself stays (nlohmann/json and tl::expected remain).

## Step 5: Update `.gitignore`

Add `.pixi/` (pixi environment cache).

## Step 6: Generate `pixi.lock`

Run `pixi install -e humble` to generate the lock file. Commit it for reproducibility.

---

## Files modified

| File | Action |
|------|--------|
| `pixi.toml` | Create |
| `CMakeLists.txt` | Edit lines 53-63 (ixwebsocket), move `include(FetchContent)` up |
| `conanfile.txt` | Add `ixwebsocket/11.4.6` |
| `.gitignore` | Add `.pixi/` |
| `3rdparty/ixwebsocket/` | Delete directory |
| `pixi.lock` | Generated |

## Verification

1. **Pixi ROS2 build**: `pixi run -e humble build` — ixwebsocket found from conda-forge, no FetchContent
2. **Pixi ROS2 test**: `pixi run -e humble test` — all 154 tests pass
3. **FastDDS Conan build**: `conan install . --output-folder=build_fastdds --build=missing && cd build_fastdds && cmake .. -DENABLE_FASTDDS=ON -DCMAKE_TOOLCHAIN_FILE=conan_toolchain.cmake && make` — ixwebsocket found from Conan
4. **Bare colcon build** (existing workflow): `colcon build --packages-select pj_bridge` — ixwebsocket fetched via FetchContent
5. Run `./run_and_test.sh` to verify regular + TSAN + ASAN all pass

The user wants to remove FetchContent entirely - no fallback. IXWebSocket must come from either pixi/conda-forge (for ROS2 builds) or Conan (for FastDDS builds). No FetchContent fallback for "bare" builds.

This means:
- For pixi builds: ixwebsocket from conda-forge ✓
- For Conan builds: ixwebsocket from conanfile.txt ✓
- For bare colcon builds (system ROS2, no pixi): `find_package(ixwebsocket REQUIRED)` will fail unless the user has ixwebsocket installed system-wide. This is acceptable - the user is saying they don't want to support that fallback path.


The CMakeLists.txt simplifies to just a required find_package call with no FetchContent fallback. Now I'm wondering whether to apply the same approach to spdlog - the user specifically mentioned ixwebsocket, but spdlog's FetchContent serves the Conan/FastDDS path where system spdlog might not be available. Since they didn't explicitly ask about spdlog, I'll leave that as is for now.

content.text: Got it — no FetchContent. Just `find_package(ixwebsocket REQUIRED)`, provided by pixi (conda-forge) or Conan depending on the build path.

# Plan: Add Pixi support (Humble/Jazzy) + Remove vendored IXWebSocket

## Context

PR #2 introduced pixi support but only for Jazzy. We want multi-environment pixi support for both Humble and Jazzy ROS2 builds, and we want to remove the vendored IXWebSocket from `3rdparty/` now that it's available on both conda-forge (v11.4.6) and Conan Center (v11.4.6).

## Step 1: Create `pixi.toml` with Humble/Jazzy environments

Create `pixi.toml` in the project root with:

- **Workspace**: `conda-forge` base channel, `linux-64` platform
- **Shared dependencies**: `cmake`, `compilers`, `pkg-config`, `ninja`, `colcon-common-extensions`, `zstd`, `spdlog`, `ixwebsocket`
- **Feature `humble`**: channel `robostack-humble`, dep `ros-humble-desktop`
- **Feature `jazzy`**: channel `robostack-jazzy`, dep `ros-jazzy-desktop`
- **Environments**: `humble = ["humble"]`, `jazzy = ["jazzy"]`
- **Activation**: `scripts = ["install/setup.sh"]` (generated after first build)
- **Tasks**: `build` (colcon build), `test` (colcon test + test-result), `clean`

Usage: `pixi run -e humble build` or `pixi run -e jazzy build`

## Step 2: Remove vendored IXWebSocket, use find_package + FetchContent fallback

**File: `CMakeLists.txt`** (lines 53-63)

Replace the `USE_VENDORED_IXWEBSOCKET` option block with the same pattern used for spdlog (lines 65-80):

```cmake
# IXWebSocket — prefer system/pixi/Conan package, FetchContent fallback for bare builds
find_package(ixwebsocket QUIET)
if(NOT ixwebsocket_FOUND)
  message(STATUS "ixwebsocket not found — fetching via FetchContent")
  FetchContent_Declare(
    ixwebsocket
    URL https://github.com/machinezone/IXWebSocket/archive/refs/tags/v11.4.6.tar.gz
    URL_HASH SHA256=<to-be-computed>
  )
  set(USE_TLS OFF CACHE BOOL "" FORCE)
  set(USE_ZLIB ON CACHE BOOL "" FORCE)
  set(IXWEBSOCKET_INSTALL OFF CACHE BOOL "" FORCE)
  FetchContent_MakeAvailable(ixwebsocket)
else()
  message(STATUS "Using system ixwebsocket")
endif()
```

Move `include(FetchContent)` above the ixwebsocket block (currently it's on line 67, between ixwebsocket and spdlog).

The target `ixwebsocket::ixwebsocket` (line 109) works in all three cases:
- **Pixi**: conda-forge `ixwebsocket` package provides cmake config
- **Conan**: CMakeDeps generates the target
- **FetchContent**: IXWebSocket's own CMakeLists.txt creates the alias

## Step 3: Add ixwebsocket to `conanfile.txt`

**File: `conanfile.txt`**

Add `ixwebsocket/11.4.6` to `[requires]` so the FastDDS Conan build finds it via `find_package`:

```
[requires]
fast-dds/3.4.0
cli11/2.6.0
ixwebsocket/11.4.6

[generators]
CMakeDeps
CMakeToolchain
```

## Step 4: Delete vendored IXWebSocket

Delete `3rdparty/ixwebsocket/` directory. The `3rdparty/` directory itself stays (nlohmann/json and tl::expected remain).

## Step 5: Update `.gitignore`

Add `.pixi/` (pixi environment cache).

## Step 6: Generate `pixi.lock`

Run `pixi install -e humble` to generate the lock file. Commit it for reproducibility.

---

## Files modified

| File | Action |
|------|--------|
| `pixi.toml` | Create |
| `CMakeLists.txt` | Edit lines 53-63 (ixwebsocket), move `include(FetchContent)` up |
| `conanfile.txt` | Add `ixwebsocket/11.4.6` |
| `.gitignore` | Add `.pixi/` |
| `3rdparty/ixwebsocket/` | Delete directory |
| `pixi.lock` | Generated |

## Verification

1. **Pixi ROS2 build**: `pixi run -e humble build` — ixwebsocket found from conda-forge, no FetchContent
2. **Pixi ROS2 test**: `pixi run -e humble test` — all 154 tests pass
3. **FastDDS Conan build**: `conan install . --output-folder=build_fastdds --build=missing && cd build_fastdds && cmake .. -DENABLE_FASTDDS=ON -DCMAKE_TOOLCHAIN_FILE=conan_toolchain.cmake && make` — ixwebsocket found from Conan
4. **Bare colcon build** (existing workflow): `colcon build --packages-select pj_bridge` — ixwebsocket fetched via FetchContent
5. Run `./run_and_test.sh` to verify regular + TSAN + ASAN all pass

## Context

PR #2 introduced pixi support but only for Jazzy. We want multi-environment pixi support for both Humble and Jazzy ROS2 builds, and we want to remove the vendored IXWebSocket from `3rdparty/` now that it's available on both conda-forge (v11.4.6) and Conan Center (v11.4.6).

- **Activation**: `scripts = ["install/setup.sh"]` (generated after first build)
- **Tasks**: `build` (colcon build), `test` (colcon test + test-result), `clean`

Usage: `pixi run -e humble build` or `pixi run -e jazzy build`

## Step 2: Remove vendored IXWebSocket, use find_package only

content.thinking:
So:
- **nlohmann_json**: Available on both conda-forge (3.12.0) and Conan (up to 3.12.0). Can be removed from 3rdparty.
- **tl-expected**: NOT on conda-forge. Available on Conan (up to 1.2.0). For pixi builds, it would need to stay vendored or we'd need another solution.

Wait — but tl::expected is a single header file. And in C++23, `std::expected` is in the standard library. Since we're using C++17, we need the tl::expected library. 


Let me double-check if tl-expected might be available on conda-forge under a different package name.

So `tl-expected` is NOT on conda-forge (only `tl-optional` is). It IS on Conan though.

For the plan:
- **nlohmann_json**: Remove from 3rdparty, add to pixi deps (conda-forge) and conanfile.txt (Conan). Both provide cmake configs.
- **tl-expected**: NOT on conda-forge. Options:
  1. Keep it vendored (it's a single header, minimal burden)
  2. Add it to pixi as a pip package? No, it's C++ headers.
  3. Create a custom conda package? Overkill for one header. 

Since tl-expected is just a single header file with no build or linking requirements, keeping it vendored makes the most sense. For Conan, I could add it to conanfile.txt, but that might complicate the include paths since Conan installs headers in a different structure than our current setup where the file lives at `3rdparty/tl/expected.hpp` and gets included via `#include "tl/expected.hpp"`.

The real constraint is that tl-expected isn't available on conda-forge, so for pixi builds we'd need to keep the vendored version anyway. The pragmatic solution is to just keep that one header file vendored and strip out everything else from 3rdparty. For nlohmann_json, even though it's also a single header, it's much larger and is available on conda-forge, so we can remove it entirely.

content.thinking:
OK so:

- **nlohmann/json**: Available on both conda-forge and Conan. Can be removed from 3rdparty. Code uses `#include <nlohmann/json.hpp>` (angle brackets), so it would work with system-installed headers.
- **tl/expected**: NOT on conda-forge. Available on Conan. Code uses `#include "tl/expected.hpp"` (quotes). Since it's a single header file and not on conda-forge, best to keep vendored.

Let me update the plan to also remove nlohmann/json from 3rdparty but keep tl/expected.
 
Actually, I need to be careful with the pixi builds—when I add `nlohmann_json` as a dependency and use `find_package(nlohmann_json)`, the CMake target will handle the include paths automatically, so the existing `#include <nlohmann/json.hpp>` should still work fine with the system-installed headers.

The tricky part is that `tl/expected` still lives in the 3rdparty directory, so I can't just remove that include path entirely. I could either keep the 3rdparty path for both libraries, or split the include directories so `nlohmann_json` comes from the CMake target while 3rdparty is only used for `tl/expected`.

Actually, since I'm deleting the nlohmann subdirectory from 3rdparty anyway, keeping the 3rdparty include path won't cause conflicts—only the tl directory will remain there. So the simplest approach is to add the find_package call and link the target while leaving the include directories unchanged.

content.text:
Results:
- **nlohmann_json**: on both conda-forge (3.12.0) and Conan (3.12.0) — **can remove**
- **tl-expected**: on Conan only, NOT on conda-forge — **keep vendored** (single header file)

Let me update the plan to also remove nlohmann/json from 3rdparty.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Plan: Add Pixi support (Humble/Jazzy) + Remove vendored IXWebSocket

## Context

PR #2 introduced pixi support but only for Jazzy. We want multi-environment pixi support for both Humble and Jazzy ROS2 builds, and we want to remove the vendored IXWebSocket from `3rdparty/` now that it's available on both conda-forge (v11.4.6) and Conan Center (v11.4.6).

## Step 1: Create `pixi.toml` with Humble/Jazzy environments

Create `pixi.toml` in the project root with:

- **Workspace**: `conda-forge` base channel, `linux-64` platform
- **Shared dependencies**: `cmake`, `compilers`, `pkg-config`, `ninja`, `colcon-common-extensions`, `zstd`, `spdlog`, `ixwebsocket`
- **Feature `humble`**: channel `robostack-humble`, dep `ros-humble-desktop`
- **Feature `jazzy`**: channel `robostack-jazzy`, dep `ros-jazzy-desktop`
- **Environments**: `humble = ["humble"]`, `jazzy = ["jazzy"]`
- **Activation**: `scripts = ["install/setup.sh"]` (generated after first build)
- **Tasks**: `build` (colcon build), `test` (colcon test + test-result), `clean`

Usage: `pixi run -e humble build` or `pixi run -e jazzy build`

## Step 2: Remove vendored IXWebSocket, use find_package only

**File: `CMakeLists.txt`** (lines 53-63)

Replace the entire `USE_VENDORED_IXWEBSOCKET` option block with a simple required find:

```cmake
# IXWebSocket — provided by pixi (conda-forge) or Conan
find_package(ixwebsocket REQUIRED)
```

Remove `include(FetchContent)` (line 67) — no longer needed (spdlog also comes from the dependency manager now; see Step 2b).

Remove the `USE_VENDORED_IXWEBSOCKET` option entirely.

The target `ixwebsocket::ixwebsocket` (line 109) works in both cases:
- **Pixi**: conda-forge `ixwebsocket` package provides cmake config
- **Conan**: CMakeDeps generates the target

### Step 2b: Also remove spdlog FetchContent

For consistency — spdlog is also on both conda-forge and Conan. Replace spdlog block (lines 65-80) with:

```cmake
find_package(spdlog REQUIRED)
```

# Plan: Add Pixi support (Humble/Jazzy) + Remove vendored IXWebSocket

## Context

PR #2 introduced pixi support but only for Jazzy. We want multi-environment pixi support for both Humble and Jazzy ROS2 builds, and we want to remove the vendored IXWebSocket from `3rdparty/` now that it's available on both conda-forge (v11.4.6) and Conan Center (v11.4.6).

## Step 1: Create `pixi.toml` with Humble/Jazzy environments

Create `pixi.toml` in the project root with:

- **Workspace**: `conda-forge` base channel, `linux-64` platform
- **Shared dependencies**: `cmake`, `compilers`, `pkg-config`, `ninja`, `colcon-common-extensions`, `zstd`, `spdlog`, `ixwebsocket`, `nlohmann_json`
- **Feature `humble`**: channel `robostack-humble`, dep `ros-humble-desktop`
- **Feature `jazzy`**: channel `robostack-jazzy`, dep `ros-jazzy-desktop`
- **Environments**: `humble = ["humble"]`, `jazzy = ["jazzy"]`
- **Activation**: `scripts = ["install/setup.sh"]` (generated after first build)
- **Tasks**: `build` (colcon build), `test` (colcon test + test-result), `clean`

Usage: `pixi run -e humble build` or `pixi run -e jazzy build`

## Step 2: Remove vendored IXWebSocket, use find_package only

**File: `CMakeLists.txt`** (lines 53-63)

Replace the entire `USE_VENDORED_IXWEBSOCKET` option block with a simple required find:


Remove `include(FetchContent)` (line 67) — no longer needed (spdlog also comes from the dependency manager now; see Step 2b).

The target `ixwebsocket::ixwebsocket` (line 109) works in both cases:
- **Pixi**: conda-forge `ixwebsocket` package provides cmake config
- **Conan**: CMakeDeps generates the target

### Step 2b: Also remove spdlog FetchContent


```
fast-dds/3.4.0
cli11/2.6.0
ixwebsocket/11.4.6
spdlog/1.15.0

[generators]
CMakeDeps
CMakeToolchain
```

## Step 4: Delete vendored IXWebSocket

Delete `3rdparty/ixwebsocket/` directory. The `3rdparty/` directory itself stays (nlohmann/json and tl::expected remain).

## Step 5: Update `.gitignore`

Add `.pixi/` (pixi environment cache).

## Step 6: Generate `pixi.lock`

Run `pixi install -e humble` to generate the lock file. Commit it for reproducibility.

---

## Files modified

| File | Action |
|------|--------|
| `pixi.toml` | Create |
| `CMakeLists.txt` | Replace ixwebsocket vendored block + spdlog FetchContent with `find_package(... REQUIRED)` |
| `conanfile.txt` | Add `ixwebsocket/11.4.6` and `spdlog/1.15.0` |
| `.gitignore` | Add `.pixi/` |
| `3rdparty/ixwebsocket/` | Delete directory |
| `pixi.lock` | Generated |

## Verification

Add `spdlog/1.15.0` to `conanfile.txt` (see Step 3).

nlohmann_json is on both conda-forge (3.12.0) and Conan (3.12.0). Add to CMakeLists.txt:

```cmake
find_package(nlohmann_json REQUIRED)
```

Add `nlohmann_json::nlohmann_json` to `target_link_libraries(pj_bridge_app ...)` (line 107-111).

Delete `3rdparty/nlohmann/` directory. Keep `3rdparty/tl/` (tl-expected is NOT on conda-forge — single header, fine to vendor).

The `3rdparty` include dir (line 104) stays — still needed for `tl/expected.hpp`.

## Step 3: Add ixwebsocket to `conanfile.txt`

Add `ixwebsocket/11.4.6`, `spdlog/1.15.0`, and `nlohmann_json/3.12.0` to `[requires]`:

```
[requires]
fast-dds/3.4.0
cli11/2.6.0
ixwebsocket/11.4.6
spdlog/1.15.0
nlohmann_json/3.12.0

[generators]
CMakeDeps
CMakeToolchain
```

## Step 4: Delete vendored IXWebSocket

Delete `3rdparty/ixwebsocket/` and `3rdparty/nlohmann/` directories. The `3rdparty/` directory stays with only `tl/expected.hpp` (not on conda-forge).

Add `.pixi/` (pixi environment cache).

Run `pixi install -e humble` to generate the lock file. Commit it for reproducibility.

# Plan: Add Pixi support (Humble/Jazzy) + Remove vendored IXWebSocket

## Context

PR #2 introduced pixi support but only for Jazzy. We want multi-environment pixi support for both Humble and Jazzy ROS2 builds, and we want to remove the vendored IXWebSocket from `3rdparty/` now that it's available on both conda-forge (v11.4.6) and Conan Center (v11.4.6).

## Step 1: Create `pixi.toml` with Humble/Jazzy environments

Create `pixi.toml` in the project root with:

- **Workspace**: `conda-forge` base channel, `linux-64` platform
- **Shared dependencies**: `cmake`, `compilers`, `pkg-config`, `ninja`, `colcon-common-extensions`, `zstd`, `spdlog`, `ixwebsocket`, `nlohmann_json`
- **Feature `humble`**: channel `robostack-humble`, dep `ros-humble-desktop`
- **Feature `jazzy`**: channel `robostack-jazzy`, dep `ros-jazzy-desktop`
- **Environments**: `humble = ["humble"]`, `jazzy = ["jazzy"]`
- **Activation**: `scripts = ["install/setup.sh"]` (generated after first build)
- **Tasks**: `build` (colcon build), `test` (colcon test + test-result), `clean`

Usage: `pixi run -e humble build` or `pixi run -e jazzy build`

## Step 2: Remove vendored IXWebSocket, use find_package only

**File: `CMakeLists.txt`** (lines 53-63)

Replace the entire `USE_VENDORED_IXWEBSOCKET` option block with a simple required find:

```cmake
# IXWebSocket — provided by pixi (conda-forge) or Conan
find_package(ixwebsocket REQUIRED)
```

Remove `include(FetchContent)` (line 67) — no longer needed (spdlog also comes from the dependency manager now; see Step 2b).

Remove the `USE_VENDORED_IXWEBSOCKET` option entirely.

The target `ixwebsocket::ixwebsocket` (line 109) works in both cases:
- **Pixi**: conda-forge `ixwebsocket` package provides cmake config
- **Conan**: CMakeDeps generates the target

### Step 2b: Also remove spdlog FetchContent

spdlog is also on both conda-forge and Conan. Replace spdlog block (lines 65-80) with:

```cmake
find_package(spdlog REQUIRED)
```

Add `spdlog/1.15.0` to `conanfile.txt` (see Step 3).

### Step 2c: Also remove nlohmann/json from 3rdparty

nlohmann_json is on both conda-forge (3.12.0) and Conan (3.12.0). Add to CMakeLists.txt:

```cmake
find_package(nlohmann_json REQUIRED)
```

Add `nlohmann_json::nlohmann_json` to `target_link_libraries(pj_bridge_app ...)` (line 107-111).

Delete `3rdparty/nlohmann/` directory. Keep `3rdparty/tl/` (tl-expected is NOT on conda-forge — single header, fine to vendor).

The `3rdparty` include dir (line 104) stays — still needed for `tl/expected.hpp`.

## Step 3: Add ixwebsocket to `conanfile.txt`

**File: `conanfile.txt`**

Add `ixwebsocket/11.4.6`, `spdlog/1.15.0`, and `nlohmann_json/3.12.0` to `[requires]`:

```
[requires]
fast-dds/3.4.0
cli11/2.6.0
ixwebsocket/11.4.6
spdlog/1.15.0
nlohmann_json/3.12.0

[generators]
CMakeDeps
CMakeToolchain
```

## Step 4: Delete vendored IXWebSocket

Delete `3rdparty/ixwebsocket/` and `3rdparty/nlohmann/` directories. The `3rdparty/` directory stays with only `tl/expected.hpp` (not on conda-forge).

## Step 5: Update `.gitignore`

Add `.pixi/` (pixi environment cache).

## Step 6: Generate `pixi.lock`

Run `pixi install -e humble` to generate the lock file. Commit it for reproducibility.

---

## Files modified

| File | Action |
|------|--------|
| `pixi.toml` | Create |
| `CMakeLists.txt` | Replace ixwebsocket/spdlog/nlohmann blocks with `find_package(... REQUIRED)` |
| `conanfile.txt` | Add `ixwebsocket/11.4.6`, `spdlog/1.15.0`, `nlohmann_json/3.12.0` |
| `.gitignore` | Add `.pixi/` |
| `3rdparty/ixwebsocket/` | Delete directory |
| `3rdparty/nlohmann/` | Delete directory |
| `pixi.lock` | Generated |

## Verification

1. **Pixi ROS2 build**: `pixi run -e humble build` — ixwebsocket found from conda-forge, no FetchContent
2. **Pixi ROS2 test**: `pixi run -e humble test` — all 154 tests pass
3. **FastDDS Conan build**: `conan install . --output-folder=build_fastdds --build=missing && cd build_fastdds && cmake .. -DENABLE_FASTDDS=ON -DCMAKE_TOOLCHAIN_FILE=conan_toolchain.cmake && make` — ixwebsocket found from Conan
4. **Bare colcon build** (existing workflow): no longer supported without pixi — deps must come from pixi or Conan
5. Run `./run_and_test.sh` from within a pixi environment to verify regular + TSAN + ASAN all pass
